endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/advisory/{advisory_id}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain an advisory in CVRF format for a given advisory ID `advisory_id` (i.e., cisco-sa-20150819-pcp)
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ADVISORY_ID}} with a real advisory_id.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/all
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all advisories in Common Vulnerability Reporting Format (CVRF). For more information about CVRF go to https://communities.cisco.com/docs/DOC-63156 . By default the output is in JSON. To obtain the output in XML use the .xml extension. For example, /advisories/cvrf/all.xml
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/cve/{cve_id}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain an advisory in CVRF format for a given Common Vulnerability Enumerator (CVE). The `cve_id` format is CVE-YYYY-NNNN. For more information about CVE visit http://cve.mitre.org/
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{CVE_ID}} with a real cve_id.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/latest/{number}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all the latest security advisories in CVRF format given an absolute number. For instance, the latest 10 or latest 5.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NUMBER}} with a real number.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/product
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all the advisories that affects the given product name.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/severity/{severity}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all security advisories for a given security impact rating (critical, high, medium, or low) in CVRF format.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SEVERITY}} with a real severity.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/severity/{severity}/firstpublished
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all security advisories for a given security impact rating (critical, high, medium, or low) in CVRF format and additionally filter based of firstpublished start date and enddate
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SEVERITY}} with a real severity.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/severity/{severity}/lastpublished
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all security advisories for a given security impact rating (critical, high, medium, or low) in CVRF format.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SEVERITY}} with a real severity.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/cvrf/year/{year}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all security advisories that have were orginally published in a specific year `YYYY`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{YEAR}} with a real year.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/ios
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all advisories that affects the given ios version
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/iosxe
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all advisories that affects the given ios version
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/advisory/{advisory_id}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain OVAL definitions for a given advisory ID `advisory_id` (i.e., cisco-sa-20150819-pcp)
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{ADVISORY_ID}} with a real advisory_id.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/all
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all Open Vulnerability and Assessment Language (OVAL) definitions available for Cisco security vulnerabilities. For more information about OVAL go to https://communities.cisco.com/docs/DOC-63158 . By default the output is in JSON. To obtain the output in XML use the .xml extension. For example, /advisories/oval/all.xml
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/cve/{cve_id}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain OVAL definitions for a given CVE Identifier. The `cve_id` format is CVE-YYYY-NNNN.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{CVE_ID}} with a real cve_id.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/latest/{number}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all the latest OVAL definitions given an absolute number. For instance, the latest 10 or latest 5.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{NUMBER}} with a real number.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/product
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all the oval advisories that affects the given product name.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/severity/{severity}
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all OVAL definitions for a given security impact rating (critical, high, medium, or low).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SEVERITY}} with a real severity.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/severity/{severity}/firstpublished
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all security advisories for a given security impact rating (critical, high, medium, or low) in OVAL format.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SEVERITY}} with a real severity.

endpoints.name: GET /security/advisories/oval/severity/{severity}/lastpublished
endpoints.description: Used to obtain all security advisories for a given security impact rating (critical, high, medium, or low) in OVAL format.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{CISCO_ACCESS_TOKEN}} with your CISCO ACCESS TOKEN. Replace {{SEVERITY}} with a real severity.
